body:

**Friend, it's hard to believe tomorrow is Election Day. We have worked for weeks to fight back against the Democrats lies and attacks and tomorrow it all comes down to voter turnout -- which is why I'm emailing you right now.**

We've set a goal of raising $100,000 today to cover the costs of our final 24 hour GOTV efforts. Your donation will help us canvass every inch of this district and could be what helps us reach that voter who pushes us over the edge to victory.

name: The White House
subject: Your 1600 Daily
body:
1600 Daily Everything White House for 6/19/2017. |
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be3965e0342f800d09d96cb46382e5ce2bea2e449e2d0ed8e56dd7b781b258c92af5e8550fe1ef8bfff1eea7acef7dcf250568a9361447d0

Today, President Donald J. Trump will host a working session with technology leaders in the private sector to develop creative solutions that will streamline government services. Modernizing government is an enormous undertaking and requires the best minds working in concert toward providing services that are effective and cost-efficient.

Today's Events

10:30 AM: President Trump receives his daily intelligence briefing

11:30 AM: President Trump and the First Lady welcome President Juan Carlos Varela and Mrs. Varela of Panama

11:35 AM: President Trump meets with President Varela

11:50 AM: President Trump has a working luncheon with President Varela

1:30 PM: Press Gaggle with Press Secretary Sean Spicer

5:00 PM: President Trump participates in an American Technology Council roundtable

6:00 PM: President Trump participates in an American Technology Council reception

Photo of the Day

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be3965e0342f800d9eab4cc4effda55df7eead1e31db4f75732db3d34fff366f97fe4e809fe4ae439faaccd127a0bfb5271cbc9e0ad65232

President Donald J. Trump signs the National Security Presidential Memorandum on Strengthening the Policy of the United States' Toward Cuba | June 16, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

WHITE HOUSE UPDATES

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be3965e0342f800dbf6c7011d4fe88bd40f16c76db3c3dc4861596236245a0df0437026c02780f733e2511208373b92be5b6d424a571937a

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be3965e0342f800d8c7312f2759caa851b44566ff42911e7c6ee374c9efde636162b9b4474434822380d371a81348efb3b6547309c9082cc
announced changes to United States' policy toward Cuba.

On Friday, President Trump
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=be3965e0342f800d3f0570213bc1c44dc89a249e458c6c70bdec0e0e2ec3a527291e5a1899e69c155799746b39e1af308e59427fcbb3b68f
approved a Kansas disaster declaration and ordered Federal assistance to supplement State and local recovery efforts.

President Trump

Last week, U.S. Secretary of Energy Rick Perry

The application period for the Fall 2017 White House Internship Program closes this Friday, June 23. Interested applicants can

apply here.

What We Are Reading

"Trump’s new measures are designed to exert more pressure on Havana to reform itself. ... Trump is right to recalibrate this policy without jettisoning it wholesale."

name: Americans for Prosperity Virginia
subject: Tomorrow: Oppose Taxpayer-Subsidized Stadiums in PWC!
body:
---
| | |
---
| | |
---|---|---
| | Peter,

Did you get my email from Saturday?
The Prince William Board of County Supervisors recently approved a letter of intent to build a baseball stadium for the minor-league "Potomac Nationals."
Just one catch: politicians and teams expect YOU to finance it! This new stadium could cost upwards of $35 million.
It's bad enough that taxpayers are on the hook to pay for a millionaire's stadium. But it's even worse that some Prince William County supervisors won't let the public vote on the matter.
[](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=74fffe679dc7c01b8592da24c55bf1b1a59a4d49785c9509498763e3b73517bacafce1798528eb850d758ac3c82a8deec41186077b3568ef)
That's why we need you to attend the Prince William Board of County Supervisors meeting on **Tuesday, June 20, at 7:00pm at the James J. McCoart Building** (1 County Complex Ct, Woodbridge, VA 22192) and tell the supervisors to let Prince William County residents voice their opinion on the taxpayer-funded stadium through public referendum.
Corporate welfare is an expensive strikeout for taxpayers; Just ask New Jersey or Connecticut residents who paid _millions_ for baseball stadiums that stand [vacant](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=74fffe679dc7c01b85dc6591b910f1b96fd9ac25974437203b98f6f8f6a978682dbec9d110c88ee8f4981606b78dd8aa231c664ff5a44037 "vacant").
Don't let Virginia be next! **Join us tomorrow**.
In the meantime, [ **tell your local officials not to subsidize stadiums**](http://click.myprosperitymail.com/?qs=74fffe679dc7c01b517b9198b3996ae1a753996164a5fd5fdce7712fa979367e66942a297883917f7bb789aecd8b1c9c4be4a12b89ec1a70 "tell your local officials not to subsidize stadiums").
For Freedom,
Tyler MuenchField DirectorAmericans for Prosperity - Virginia

name: Team Kaine
subject: utter disaster
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek --

President Trump recently released his proposal for America's budget.

**It 's an utter disaster.**
* $1.3 trillion in cuts to Medicaid (despite his campaign promise!)
* $193 billion in cuts to food stamps
* $72 billion in cuts to disability programs including Social Security
* $5 billion in cuts by eliminating public education programs

That's not all. The Trump budget would also slash the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), medical research, funding to address the opioid epidemic, and much more. And it would _eliminate_ the Economic Development Administration, the EPA's Chesapeake Bay Program, and the Appalachian Regional Commission, which partners with Appalachian localities to build roads, replace water pipes, create jobs, lay broadband fiber, promote tourism, set up workforce initiatives at community colleges, and many other things.

**In other words, this is a budget that would do a great deal of harm to Virginians.**

As a member of the Senate Budget Committee, Tim is set on passing a budget that will help Virginians thrive. **[Will you take our team's brief survey on how the Trump budget could affect you and your family?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=iaHE77GAyfZSFzGraxm%2Fh7HEcNdRgBjm)**

Thanks,
Team Kaine

| Friends, if we don't act quickly, a nightmare for Florida is just a matter of time.

Florida Governor Rick Scott refused to speak out against President Trump's withdrawal from the Paris Climate Agreement.

Now, Trump has proposed massive cuts to disaster preparedness, hurricane research, and emergency relief. If Trump gets his way FEMA would lose $667 million, the National Hurricane Center's budget would shrink by $114 million, and the Pre-Disaster Mitigation Grant Program would be slashed by a staggering 61 percent.

Rick Scott's response to the gutting of programs vital to Florida communities: **silence.**

Florida deserves leadership that will stand up and speak out for the best interests of our state. [_**Demand Trump and Rick Scott take action to address the effects of climate change rather than cut millions of dollars from hurricane research and disaster relief.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113efe/622b7b5b/3bdb8bf/307bc68a/1322347214/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170611")

This hurricane season is expected to be busier than usual and Florida cannot afford major cuts to hurricane research as well as disaster preparedness and relief. Silence and inaction will not defend Florida communities against a changing climate and ravaging storms.

[_**Add your name and demand that Trump and Rick Scott take action on climate change instead of ducking from responsibility.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113efe/622b7b5b/3bdb8bf/307bc68a/1322347214/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170611")

name: DEVELOPING (via DSCC)
subject: Democrats are holding the Senate floor tonight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Today, Democrats are going all in: our senators are planning on holding the floor tonight to protest the GOP's Obamacare repeal bill.
---
**[](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e5f5/2b07114c/2d570edc/73108455/2586891329/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-trumpcare-fast-track?source=PT-20170619-DevelopingDS-ProsHV")**
**_CNN_ : Dems to Block Senate Business Over GOP Secrecy on Health Care**
Team,

Today, Democrats are going all in: our senators are planning on holding the floor tonight to protest the GOP's Obamacare repeal bill.

We've had enough of the secrecy and the closed door meetings surrounding this bill. Mitch McConnell is bending Senate rules to push through repeal as quickly as possible. His behavior is not only reckless, but heartless and unfair to the American people.

The reality is that millions of lives are at stake, and that's why our senators are literally standing up and fighting to protect our care.

[**Show you 're standing with Democrats tonight to protest the GOP's attempt to secretly rush through this disastrous bill. Add your name now to oppose Obamacare repeal.**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e5f5/2b07114c/2d570edc/73108455/2586891329/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stop-trumpcare-fast-track?source=PT-20170619-DevelopingDS-ProsHV")

The political maneuvers Republicans are pulling to ram through this bill are unprecedented -- making it clear that we have to take bold action to make them listen.

So if Democratic senators have to hold the floor late in to the night to disrupt their tactics and stall business as usual, they will.

name: Karen
subject: Before I go to sleep, I wanted to send you this email
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Handel for Congress, Inc
body:
Handel for Congress

Friend, before I head to sleep, I wanted to send you this
personal note.

Friend, before I go to bed tonight (if I'm able to sleep!), I
just wanted to send you a note to say Thank You.

I know tomorrow is going to be pretty hectic which is why I
wanted to write you now.

This campaign has been nothing short of incredible -- and I
wouldn't have been able to run such a strong campaign without
your support. It was from you and many other like-minded
conservatives that we were able to push back against the
Democrats (who spent millions and millions against us) and run a
fair and positive campaign. I'm proud of the campaign we've run
and I hope you are too.

We still have our work cut out for us before the polls close, but
I wanted you to know that I wouldn't be here tonight, the night
before Election Day in a statistical dead heat if it weren't for
you.

You helped make this race so great. And I will forever be
grateful to you, Friend.

Thank you for standing up for our campaign and our future.

Now let's go WIN!

- Karen

name: Jenna Kruse, EMILY's List
subject: What happens when you elect pro-choice Democratic women >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7a07/24c69fd9/da3e33a8/18e78a7a/2197169456/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact&source=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact")
---

Peter,

The Trump administration is determined to make contraception expensive and inaccessible, but our women at the state and local level are resisting.

Last year, we flipped the Nevada state legislature from red to blue and swept a slate of pro-choice Democratic women into office.

Now, those victories are paying dividends. Nevada's governor just signed into law legislation to protect and expand access to birth control -- and the bill's chief architects were two EMILY's List women, Assemblywoman Teresa Benitez-Thompson and State Sen. Julia Ratti.

Thanks to their work, Nevada women will soon be able to pick up 12 months of birth control at a time, helping to reduce gaps between usage and thus reducing unplanned pregnancies.

**[Donate $3 to support pro-choice Democratic women at the state and local level as they form a firewall against Trump's attacks on women's health care.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7a07/24c69fd9/da3e33a8/18e78a7b/2197169456/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170619_email_ProactiveStateWomen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+support+prochoice+Democratic&utm_campaign=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact&source=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact")**

Recently, a Trump proposal leaked that would let more employers decide if workers can access birth control. It's an outrageous, blatant attack on women.

That's why EMILY's List women around the country, like Teresa and Julia, have been taking proactive steps to protect our health care.

In Oregon, State Sen. Laurie Monnes Anderson is moving a bill that would protect no-copay birth control and require health insurers to cover a full range of well-woman care including prenatal care, breastfeeding support, STI testing, and abortion care.

And in New Mexico, State Rep. Debbie Armstrong sponsored a bill to make the birth control mandate the law of the land in her state, regardless of whether Trump overturns the rule or not.

THIS is the kind of take-charge action we get if we have pro-choice Democratic women at every level of government.

**Donate $3 to continue our work electing resistant and persistent women at the state and local level:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/State-Candidates-Step-Up](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7a07/24c69fd9/da3e33a8/18e78a74/2197169456/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170619_email_ProactiveStateWomen?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgStateCandidatesS&utm_campaign=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact&source=WQEFE624N_digital_email_20170619_ProactiveStateWomen_inact")**

Thanks,

Jenna Kruse
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

But with the Obamacare repeal vote rumored to be coming as soon as next week, we need to push this initiative even further. That's why we're coming to you.
